narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'climate cultists' and implying that their language is 'bizarre' and scare-mongering.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by suggesting that past predictions have been incorrect and that the current warnings are exaggerated.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of climate science by highlighting the alleged failure of past predictions, implying that the science is unreliable.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent climate advocate, by implying that he is hypocritical and that his personal actions contradict his public statements on climate change.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Unless America and all other sovereign nations open up their borders to an illegal alien onslaught, the climate crisis will only continue to worsen, Gore went on to allege.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text implies that there is a hidden plot to use the climate crisis as a pretext for opening borders and undermining national sovereignty, suggesting a secret scheme by powerful groups.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text attacks the climate movement by using pejorative language, such as 'climate cultists', and mocking their terminology, indicating a criticism of the movement's messaging and tactics.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by suggesting that past predictions have been incorrect, implying that current warnings are also exaggerated or unfounded.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of climate science by highlighting the alleged failure of past predictions, casting doubt on the reliability of climate models and data.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent climate advocate, for his personal actions, implying hypocrisy and challenging his credibility on climate issues, which reflects a criticism of institutions and authorities promoting climate action.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text attacks the climate movement by using pejorative language, such as 'climate cultists', and mocking their terminology, implying that they are using scare tactics to manipulate the public.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: “Renewable energy ... is now the cheapest source of electricity in almost the entire planet,” Gore lied.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of Gore's statement about renewable energy, implying that the science and data are being misrepresented or manipulated.
